senses.definition: someone whose job involves performing in movies, plays, or series

examples.x: Acting classes help aspiring actors develop their skills and techniques.
Many actors dream of winning prestigious awards for their performances.
The actor's ability to connect with the audience on an emotional level creates a memorable performance.
The director praised the actor for their outstanding performance in the movie.
The talented actor effortlessly portrayed a wide range of characters, from a hero to a villain.

senses.definition: a woman whose job involves performing in movies, plays, or series

examples.x: I saw a famous actress at the shopping mall today.
I want to be an actress when I grow up.
The actress performed beautifully in the play.
The actress was practicing her lines before the audition.
The young actress received an award for her outstanding performance.

senses.definition: a person who substitutes for an actor in a film, typically during scenes that involves nude or dangerous scenes
a group of actors who share roughly equal screen time and importance in a movie
an actor who always plays the role of a bizarre or outlandish character rather than a main role
an actor who plays the main male role in a movie or play
an actress who plays the main role in a movie or play
an actor with an exaggerated theatrical style
an actor who plays the main role in a play or movie

examples.x: The lead captivated audiences with his powerful performance in the title role of the play.
She auditioned for the lead in the school musical and was thrilled to be cast as the lead singer.
The director praised the lead for her dedication and talent in bringing the protagonist to life on screen.
He dreamed of becoming a lead in a Hollywood blockbuster and spent years honing his craft as an actor.
The lead's charisma and stage presence commanded attention whenever he stepped onto the stage.

senses.definition: an actor who plays a secondary character in a film or television show, often providing support or context to the main plot or protagonist
a performer who provides voices for animated films, TV shows, video games, commercials, audiobooks, and other media where speaking voices are needed
a minor role in a movie or play
a person who replaces someone else briefly in doing their job while they are not available

examples.x: She worked as a stand-in for the CEO during his business trip.
He served as a stand-in for his friend at the meeting while she dealt with a personal emergency.
The photographer used a stand-in to test the lighting before the main model arrived.
The actor was sick, so the director asked the understudy to act as a stand-in for the rehearsal.

senses.definition: the chief actor or performer in a motion picture, play, TV or radio program, etc.

examples.x: She became a star after her role in the hit movie.
The star of the play received a standing ovation.
He was the star of a popular TV series.
The director praised the star for an outstanding performance.
As the star of the show, she had the most lines and scenes.

senses.definition: a young and promising female actor who is coached and publicized in order to become a star
an actor who takes part in performing a role in a tragedy
an actor who practices the lines of another actor in order to replace them if necessary

examples.x: The understudy stepped into the lead role at the last minute when the main actor fell ill.
The director praised the understudy's dedication during rehearsals.
The understudy had to memorize the script quickly when called upon to perform.
She diligently prepared as the understudy for the protagonist in the upcoming play.
He was surprised but ready when asked to take over as understudy for the lead role.

senses.definition: a small, non-speaking role played by an actor who appears briefly on screen, often as a background character or extra
a professional who trains actors to improve their performance skills and technique
a character in a story or film who is portrayed as an antagonist or villain

examples.x: The bad guy in the movie had a sinister plan.
He always plays the bad guy in action films.
The bad guy was finally defeated in the final scene.
Some stories make the bad guy more complex and relatable.
The audience cheered when the hero outsmarted the bad guy.

senses.definition: a minor role that is played by a well-known actor

examples.x: The audience was delighted when the famous actor made a surprise cameo in the latest superhero movie, appearing briefly as a witty bartender.
Her cameo in the romantic comedy, though only a few minutes long, stole the show and left a lasting impression on viewers.
The director included a subtle cameo by a beloved veteran actor, paying homage to the classic films that inspired the new production.
The singer's cameo in the TV series added an extra layer of excitement, with fans thrilled to see their favorite performer in an unexpected acting role.

senses.definition: a leading actor or actress who takes part in a movie, play, etc.
a person hired to appear in a film or television production, typically in the background of scenes to add realism

examples.x: The film's production team needed several extras to fill the bustling city street scenes.
She worked as an extra on the set, blending into the background of the busy café scene.
Being an extra in the film gave him a brief glimpse of the glamorous world of movie-making.

senses.definition: a protagonist or a heroic character in a story or film who embodies positive traits and intentions
the specific role given to an actor
the part or character that an actor plays in a movie or play

examples.x: He played the role of a detective in the crime thriller.
I auditioned for a small role in the school drama.
I'm excited about my new role in the upcoming musical.
She was praised for her role in the new film.

senses.definition: a lead or main role played by an actor in a film or a theatrical production
the main character in a production whose name is also the title of the film, television show, or play
the main bad character in a movie, story, play, etc.

examples.x: The villain plotted to take over the kingdom.
He played the villain in the new action film.
The villain’s motives were revealed at the end of the story.
A good villain makes the story more exciting.
The audience booed when the villain appeared on stage.

senses.definition: the words recited by an actor in a play or movie
a few words or actions that hint another performer to say a line or do something

examples.x: The actor waited for his cue before entering the stage.
The director gave a subtle nod as a cue for the lights to dim.
She missed her cue and entered the scene at the wrong moment.
His cue came just as the audience held their breath in anticipation.
During rehearsals, the actors practiced responding to each other's cues.

senses.definition: a word or phrase that an actor says to signal another actor to begin acting or say a line
a text in the script of a play, giving an instruction regarding the movement, position, etc. of actors

examples.x: The stage direction called for a prop to be placed center stage before the next scene.
In the script, the stage direction specified a sudden blackout to create suspense.
During rehearsal, the actors practiced the intricate choreography as per the stage direction.
The stage direction advised the actor to pause for dramatic effect before delivering the punchline.
The stage direction instructed the actors to exit quietly, leaving the audience in suspense.

senses.definition: the process of assigning roles and parts to actors or performers in a movie, play, etc.

examples.x: The casting for the new film involved auditions with hundreds of actors vying for roles.
The director's vision for the film was realized through meticulous casting, with actors selected to embody specific characters.

senses.definition: a session of audition during which the actor is recorded in order to be assessed for a role
a main part of a play, opera, or ballet

examples.x: The first act of the play introduces the main characters and their conflicts.
Each act of the opera showcased the talents of the performers.
The final act of the ballet featured an impressive display of choreography.
The actors rehearsed diligently to perfect their performances for each act.
After the intermission, the audience eagerly anticipated the second act.

senses.definition: an evil character in fiction or a motion picture
an actor's line that is told to the audience but the other characters on the stage are not intended to hear

examples.x: The villain delivered an aside that hinted at his next sinister move.
Shakespeare often uses the aside to let the audience in on secrets the rest of the cast doesn't know.

senses.definition: the main character in a movie, novel, TV show, etc.

examples.x: The protagonist of the novel is a young orphan who embarks on a journey of self-discovery and adventure.
In the film, the protagonist's unwavering determination and courage inspire those around them to rise up against injustice.
The protagonist's internal conflict drives the narrative forward as they grapple with difficult choices and confront their own flaws and insecurities.
As the story unfolds, the protagonist undergoes a transformative journey, evolving from a reluctant hero into a symbol of hope and resilience.
The protagonist's quest for redemption and forgiveness forms the emotional core of the narrative, resonating with audiences on a deeply human level.

senses.definition: a written or spoken line that is spoken by a character in a play, movie, book, or other work of fiction

examples.x: The dialogue between the two characters revealed their hidden motives.
She spent hours perfecting the dialogue for her screenplay.
The play’s dialogue was both witty and thought-provoking.
His dialogue in the scene felt natural and engaging.
The actors rehearsed their dialogue repeatedly before opening night.

senses.definition: an act of departure from the stage by an actor
someone who is morally good, especially a character in a movie, play or book
the main male character in a story, book, movie, etc., often known for his bravery and other great qualities

examples.x: Every superhero movie needs a strong hero.
The hero fights the villain in an epic battle.
The hero of the movie was a courageous firefighter.
The hero saves the day in the final scene.
The story follows the hero's transformation from a farmer to a knight.

senses.definition: the main female character in a story, book, film, etc., typically known for great qualities

examples.x: She admired the bravery of the heroine in the novel.
The film features a heroine who stands up for what she believes in.
The heroine of the book is a young girl with special abilities.
The heroine of the play is a strong and determined woman.
The story is about a heroine who fights evil with her magical powers.

senses.definition: a person who is romantically or emotionally involved with another person, often a central character in a story or narrative

examples.x: The movie's love interest was a mysterious stranger with a hidden past.
His love interest in the book was a strong, independent character.
They debated whether the hero's love interest was necessary for the plot.
The director cast a famous actress as the main character's love interest.
In the play, the love interest added emotional depth to the protagonist's journey.

senses.definition: a speech that a character in a dramatic play gives in the form of a monologue as a series of inner reflections spoken out loud

examples.x: The actor delivered the soliloquy with intensity, allowing the audience to glimpse the character's innermost thoughts and emotions.
Shakespeare's plays are renowned for their soliloquies, in which characters reveal their deepest desires and fears through introspective monologues.
The soliloquy served as a window into the character's psyche, shedding light on their motivations and internal struggles.
In the midst of turmoil, the protagonist delivered a powerful soliloquy, grappling with conflicting emotions and existential questions.
The soliloquy provided a moment of introspection and revelation, drawing the audience into the character's inner world and inviting empathy and understanding.

senses.definition: a fictional character with special and strange powers
a role or part played by an actor, performer, voice actor, etc.

examples.x: Robert Downey Jr. portrayed the character of Tony Stark in Iron Man.
Meryl Streep took on the character of Margaret Thatcher in The Iron Lady.
The actor playing the character of Hamlet received critical acclaim for his performance.
The voice actor brought the character of Shrek to life in the animated film.
Tom Hanks played the character of Forrest Gump in the movie of the same name.

senses.definition: a wardrobe assistant for an actor
someone who manages a theater company and also acts in their plays

examples.x: As an actor-manager, he not only starred in productions but also handled administrative duties and managed the theater company.
The tradition of the actor-manager dates back to the 18th century when performers took on managerial roles in addition to their acting responsibilities.
She thrived in her role as an actor-manager, balancing the demands of performance with the challenges of running a theater.
Actor-managers played a pivotal role in shaping the theater industry, influencing repertoire choices, casting decisions, and production styles.
He faced the pressures of multitasking as an actor-manager, often juggling rehearsals, performances, and business meetings.

senses.definition: all the actors and actresses in a movie, play, etc.

examples.x: She was excited to join the cast of the upcoming film.
The director introduced the cast during the press conference.
A talented cast can make even a simple story compelling.
The cast worked together for months to perfect their performances.
An all-star cast was chosen for the high-budget movie.

senses.definition: a good-looking actor who is admired by women
a famous actor or actress who plays the leading role in a movie
an actor specially one who performs a role on a stage
someone who reminds actors what to say if they forget their lines on the stage
a person who performs dangerous or difficult actions in place of actors in movies or shows

examples.x: The stuntman practiced his fall from the horse all day.
She hired a stuntman to perform the risky motorcycle stunt.
The stuntman jumped off a building for the action scene.
During the chase scene, the stuntman had to leap between moving cars.
The stuntman wore protective gear while doing the fight scene.

senses.definition: a woman who doubles for an actor during the production of dangerous scenes in a movie

examples.x: The stunt woman executed a flawless high-speed car chase for the movie.
She trained for years to become a professional stunt woman.
The stunt woman jumped from the building without hesitation.
Directors often rely on a skilled stunt woman for action sequences.
The stunt woman’s dedication to safety ensured the scene went smoothly.

senses.definition: an actor or performer who is very reliable and has a lot of experience
someone who is in charge of the advertising and publicity of a particular actor, musician, etc., providing information to a newspaper, magazine, etc.
an extended speech delivered by an actor within a play or film

examples.x: The actress received a standing ovation for her moving monologue in the third act of the play.
In the climactic scene of the movie, the protagonist's monologue revealed his innermost conflicts and resolutions.

senses.definition: a line that is recited in a speech or performance without prior preparation

examples.x: The actor's quick wit saved the scene when he forgot his line, delivering a perfectly timed ad lib that had the audience roaring with laughter.
Known for her improvisational skills, the comedian often peppered her routine with ad libs, making each performance unique and spontaneous.
During the live broadcast, the anchor handled an unexpected technical glitch with a smooth ad lib, maintaining the show's flow seamlessly.
The director encouraged the cast to experiment with ad libs during rehearsals, hoping to discover fresh and authentic moments to enhance the play.
The singer's charming ad lib between verses added a personal touch to the concert, engaging the audience and making them feel part of the performance.

senses.definition: the techniques used by actors to develop and portray a character, including their physicality, personality, and backstory
a performer who appears on television, film, or other visual media
